channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Queensland premier learned of minister's AFP referral through media
item.description: Questions have been raised about how long Tim Mander waited to tell the Queensland premier about his referral to the AFP.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: David Crisafulli's office found out about the referral of his Olympics minister to federal police via the media.

item.title: Law allowing flooding on private land 'critical' for environment, expert says
item.description: Controversial new legislation could secure critical water supplies for delicate ecosystems while delivering benefits to the Murray-Darling Basin's agricultural sector, a leading river ecologist says.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter

media:group.media:description: Agriculture and environmental ecosystems exist side by side across the Murray-Darling Basin.

item.title: In 1990 there were 599 million mental disorder cases globally. Now there are 1.2 billion
item.description: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Morris-Grant

media:group.media:description: The number of mental health cases worldwide has almost doubled over the past three decades, according to new research published this week.

item.title: What is 'pink slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?
item.description: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.
item.dc:creator: Madigan Landry

media:group.media:description: The Bunbury Guardian is one of a series of AI generated news sites which appeared in regional WA earlier this year.

item.title: 'Absolute shock': NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies aged 41
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won two Cup Series championships and will be a future NASCAR Hall of Famer, has died aged 41.

media:group.media:description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch has died aged 41 after being admitted to hospital this morning.

item.title: DNA study finds many dingoes may have some domestic dog genetics
item.description: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.
item.dc:creator: Angus Verley

media:group.media:description: The study found many dingoes previously considered purebred have domestic dog ancestry.

item.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up skies across NSW
item.description: Colourful streaks across the skies have been spotted from Bondi to Canberra as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere overnight.
item.dc:creator: Miriah Davis and Julia André

media:group.media:description: Footage showing a meteor in the sky over Jugiong last night.

item.title: US-bound plane diverted after passenger from Ebola-hit Congo boards 'in error'
item.description: The US has introduced new restrictions on travellers from countries exposed to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, requiring them to enter through Washington DC.

media:group.media:description: Air France planes at the Charles de Gaulle airport (file photo).

item.title: Arsenal coach cooked a barbecue instead of watching title-deciding game
item.description: Mikel Arteta says he decided to not watch the match that secured Arsenal an historic EPL title, instead deciding to cook a barbecue.

media:group.media:description: Mikel Arteta said he could not watch the decisive game, so instead cooked a barbecue.

item.title: Former NT political staffer claims unfair dismissal, airs bullying allegations
item.description: A former staffer for Northern Territory government backbencher Clinton Howe is claiming she was sacked for refusing to falsify constituent information in a database, and was bullied and micromanaged by the politician's full-time electorate officer.
item.dc:creator: Jack Hislop

media:group.media:description: Clinton Howe's electoral office is located in the Gateway Shopping Centre in Palmerston.

item.title: Arsonists smash their way into Melbourne luxury car dealership
item.description: Arsonists struck a luxury car dealership in Melbourne's inner-east overnight, smashing into the building and setting it alight.

media:group.media:description: The dealership in Richmond following the attack.

item.title: What's going wrong inside the City of Perth?
item.description: Bruce Reynolds is not shedding any light on the issues at hand in one of the lord mayor's first interviews since the City of Perth's CEO was suspended from duties and the council was formally put on notice.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Withers

media:group.media:description: Lord Mayor Bruce Reynolds is steering the City of Perth through turbulent times, but questions remain unanswered.

item.title: What you might not know about Pendlebury's lauded basketball background
item.description: Scott Pendlebury is about to set the record for most AFL matches played. This is the latest milestone in a stellar career that almost never was.
item.dc:creator: Bec Symons

media:group.media:description: Scott Pendlebury has carved out a record-breaking career since being drafted in 2005.

item.title: Live: ASX to climb, oil drops on US–Iran deal hopes
item.description: Australian markets are set to rise this morning, as oil falls on hopes of a diplomatic way out of the war in Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller and Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: Live: Plibersek says second IS-linked group could face 'full force of the law'
item.description: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek says a group of Islamic State-linked Australians who have left a camp in Syria will face the "full force of the law" if they are suspected of committing crimes overseas. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Tanya Plibersek launches the National Circular Economy Framework at Sircel in Villawood, NSW.

item.title: Berlin approves bid to host Olympics on 100th anniversary of Nazi-run Games
item.description: Berlin's state parliament approves a bid to host the Olympics in 2036, on the 100-year anniversary of the Games hosted by Adolf Hitler and the Nazis.

media:group.media:description: American sprinter Jesse Owens speaks with German officials after claiming gold in the 200m at the 1936 Berlin Olympics.

item.title: Arrest of alleged teen car thieves in Melbourne's west caught on camera
item.description: Police make 35 arrests in Melbourne's west as they target record-high car thefts across Victoria.

media:group.media:description: Five teenagers were arrested after fleeing an allegedly stolen vehicle in Melbourne's west.

item.title: Four-metre waves on the way as south-east Queensland braces for beach erosion
item.description: Large swells driven by a Tasman Sea low are expected to bring hazardous surf and further erosion to already affected Gold Coast beaches over the weekend, before easing early next week.
item.dc:creator: Dominic Cansdale and Charmayne Allison

media:group.media:description: A "no swimming" sign at Burleigh Heads on Thursday ahead of forecast hazardous coastal conditions.

item.title: Parents of children in foster care say 'black eyes' and bite marks were ignored
item.description: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: The parents of two children placed in the Northern Territory's foster care system have spoken out ahead of planned changes to child protection laws.

item.title: Half Australia's farms are in trusts. So what does tax reform mean for farmers?
item.description: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.
item.dc:creator: David Claughton, Warwick Long, and Joshua Becker

media:group.media:description: Farming income has been excluded from the 30 per cent minimum tax on family trusts.

item.title: Dismay over 150-year-old house being reduced to rubble
item.description: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Reid

media:group.media:description: The recently demolished Launceston home was built in the 1870s.

item.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says
item.description: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

media:group.media:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says a negotiated deal between the US and Cuba is preferred but unlikely.

item.title: Sydney council slaps dog walkers and gender reveals with new park fees
item.description: A picnic vendor told the ABC they found out about the proposed fees through a group chat with other small businesses, and fears the proposed fee structure could "kill an entire industry".
item.dc:creator: Miriah Davis

media:group.media:description: Pop-up picnic vendors will soon have to pay to use parks in one cash-strapped Sydney council.

item.title: Optus planned for 13-day outage but forgot to tell town's customers
item.description: Optus has apologised after failing to notify a western Riverina town about a 13-day planned outage for service upgrades.
item.dc:creator: Nicola Ceccato

media:group.media:description: Lorraine Withers is waiting on an important phone call from her doctor.

item.title: Why consumers are forking out more for whiting, calamari and garfish
item.description: South Australia's peak seafood body says a short supply of some species due to the algal bloom and commercial fishing restrictions has led to slight price increases, as it warns it could take industry five to 10 years to fully recover.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Merritt

media:group.media:description: King George Whiting, calamari and garfish are slightly more expensive in Adelaide markets.
